mission.title: Install and Configure Weaviate
mission.description: Weaviate is an open-source vector database that combines vector search with structured filtering, making it ideal for applications like semantic search and recommendation engines. It is cloud-native, offering scalability and fault tolerance, but requires careful resource management in production environments.

steps.title: Add Weaviate Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Weaviate Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add weaviate https://weaviate.io/helm

```

> This URL points to the official Weaviate Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install Weaviate CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Weaviate to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weaviate/weaviate/main/deploy/crds/weaviate.io_weaviates.yaml

```

> This command fetches and applies the CRDs from the Weaviate GitHub repository.

steps.title: Install Weaviate using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Weaviate in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install weaviate weaviate/weaviate --namespace weaviate --create-namespace --version 1.36.0

```

> This installs Weaviate with the specified version in a new namespace.

steps.title: Create a Weaviate instance
steps.description:
This step creates an example Weaviate instance to verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: weaviate.io/v1
kind: Weaviate
metadata:
  name: example-weaviate
spec:
  version: "1.36.0"
EOF

```

> This command creates a Weaviate instance using a YAML definition.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: weaviate.io/v1
kind: Weaviate
metadata:
  name: example-weaviate
spec:
  version: "1.36.0"
EOF

steps.title: Verify Weaviate instance
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Weaviate instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get weaviate -n weaviate

```

> This command lists the Weaviate instances in the specified namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Weaviate
uninstall.description:
This step removes Weaviate and its associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall weaviate --namespace weaviate

kubectl delete namespace weaviate

```

> This command uninstalls Weaviate from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Weaviate
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step upgrades Weaviate to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get weaviate -n weaviate -o yaml > weaviate-backup.yaml

helm upgrade weaviate weaviate/weaviate --namespace weaviate --version 1.36.0

kubectl get weaviate -n weaviate

```

> Backup the current Weaviate instance configuration.
> This command upgrades Weaviate to the specified version.
> Verify that the Weaviate instance is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get weaviate -n weaviate -o yaml > weaviate-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Weaviate instance is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Weaviate instance may not have started correctly due to insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Weaviate'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Weaviate service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service is correctly configured and accessible from your network.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions may not have been applied correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weaviate/weaviate/main/deploy/crds/weaviate.io_weaviates.yaml.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC settings may not allow the current user to access Weaviate resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the user has the necessary permissions to access the Weaviate resources.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vectorization models and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old vectorization API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.35.0, update your API calls to use the new endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see the Weaviate instance listed with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get weaviate -n weaviate'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: weaviate.io/v1
kind: Weaviate
metadata:
  name: example-weaviate
spec:
  version: "1.36.0"
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
